about.title: A studio built on focus and community
about.intro1: Gioia Reformer Studio opened in March 2025. Although reformer pilates was already gaining popularity at the time, Pula didn't yet have a studio offering this type of training.
about.intro2: Our aim was to introduce people to the reformer and its benefits for each individual, and to create a space where anyone who wants to prioritise their health and improve their physical condition is welcome, in a supportive and positive environment.
about.intro3: We believe learning is a continuous process, and since we opened our doors, we have grown and developed alongside our clients.
about.intro4: Gioia isn't made by us alone; it's made by the people who walk through our doors.
about.valuesIntro: From day one, we have held to a set of guiding principles:
about.manifesto01Text: Undivided attention on each client. We have 6 reformers, and we believe that's the right number to observe and correct every client's form with genuine care and precision.
about.manifesto02Text: We track each client's progress and celebrate every step forward, no matter how big or small.
about.manifesto03Text: We respect every client in our studio and genuinely value every piece of feedback.
about.manifesto04Text: The energy and sense of community that grows inside the studio matters deeply to us. We make sure everyone always feels seen, safe, and supported.

classes.forWho1Text: A safe, guided introduction to movement with expert instruction
classes.forWho2Text: Relief for tension in the back, neck, and shoulders
classes.forWho3Text: Deepening control, strength, and body awareness
classes.forWho4Text: A calm yet intense workout that connects body and mind
classes.trainingTitle: Three concepts. One goal. A strong and mindful body.
classes.trainingIntro: All of our sessions last 55 minutes, including a warm-up and cool-down alongside the main workout. We regularly incorporate props such as dumbbells, glute bands, and hand weights. Each session works the whole body, though the reformer is particularly effective at building core strength. You'll need non-slip socks, a small towel, and water, and you're ready.
classes.strengthDesc: This is what we'd call our signature class. We started with just this format, but over time we found clients needed more variety. The session blends strength exercises with traditional, foundational reformer work. It's challenging, but everyone handles it well. Alongside the reformer, we use free weights, ankle weights, a pilates ball and ring, resistance bands, and a bar. The goal is to build strength with a focus on the core, improving stability, posture, and the quality of everyday movement.
classes.stretchDesc: A gentle reformer class suited to beginners, those new to the reformer, those returning from a break, or anyone with mild spinal concerns. The pace is relaxed, with an emphasis on stretching, following the teachings of Joseph Pilates. The session focuses on deep muscle work, spinal support and lengthening, and improving overall body alignment, in a calm, considered way.
classes.cardioDesc: A dynamic, fast-paced class for more experienced participants. Using a jumping board (a small trampoline-style attachment on the reformer), you can experience high-intensity interval training with a focus on the lower body, in a fun and engaging way. We recommend this class for clients who have been training on the reformer for some time or are already in good physical condition.
classes.pricingNote: Depending on the type of training and how often you'd like to come, we offer a range of packages and memberships. We're happy to help you find the option that suits you best.
classes.individualSub: Personalised attention, complete focus on you
classes.groupSub: Up to 6 participants: group energy with personal attention

team.title: The trainers guiding your transformation
team.intro: At Gioia, we believe the relationship between trainer and client is one of the most important elements of progress. Everyone who comes to us receives support, clear guidance, and a sense of safety, without rush, comparison, or pressure.
team.sanjaP1: Gioia is a studio that reflects who I am and what I believe in. It was created to bring confidence, support (both physical and personal) and to hold space for those who want that little bit more: strength, calm, and a positive environment. A place where people can focus entirely on themselves and leave feeling recharged. I've been passionate about pilates and training for years, and I hold a deep love and respect for movement and the body, something we so often take for granted. No plant can survive without care and water, and the same is true of our bodies. They are strong and powerful, but neglect them and you'll feel it over time. Your body is your home. Take care of it.
team.sanjaP2: I first encountered pilates almost twenty years ago, when it was just beginning to appear in Croatia, though nowhere near as popular as it is today. I practiced it while living abroad and always found it both intense and captivating. In pilates, the smallest movements create the greatest change. To slow down is to take control. By connecting breath and movement, we activate energy and bring the body into psychophysical balance, and in those 55 minutes, you have complete connection with yourself.
team.sanjaP3: I was always searching for a space that offered everything I now offer in my own studio, which is why it was important from the very beginning to create somewhere that wouldn't be just another studio, but a place where we could all grow together and be a source of support for one another. I'm genuinely happy, proud, and grateful that others have felt that too.
team.teaP1: A degree in Japanese studies brought me to Pula, and after graduating I found myself at one of Sanja's classes, where I discovered something I could truly live and love: my ikigai.
team.teaP2: Movement has always been my way of finding clarity and a sense of control. Pilates became my source of stability and presence. Through years of practice, I've come to understand how the body responds to different movements and what it means to train with real awareness, knowledge I deepened under Asja Petersen, founder of the first pilates studio in Croatia.
team.teaP3: I bring that practice and understanding into every session. My aim is for clients to feel safe, grounded, and a little stronger than when they arrived.
team.teaP4: In short, being a pilates instructor is the natural continuation of everything I value: self-care, learning, and growth.
team.teaP5: Whatever brings you to reformer pilates, at Gioia we'll start exactly where you are and take you exactly where you want to go.

reviews.title: What our clients say
reviews.t1: Both trainers are wonderfully skilled and calming. The exercises are clearly explained and every movement feels intentional, and I feel so much more confident in my body.
reviews.t2: Beautiful studio, an intimate atmosphere, and genuinely attentive instruction. After just a few sessions I feel so much less tension in my back.
reviews.t3: I love that groups are small and that someone actually corrects you when needed. I leave every session feeling stronger and calmer.
reviews.t4: The space is beautiful and peaceful, and the sessions are challenging in the best way. Perfect after a long day at work.

press.title: In the Press
press.istarskiDesc: A full feature on the studio's founder and concept, from life in Vienna and Melbourne to creating an intimate reformer studio in Pula.
press.stylishDesc: The studio is highlighted as a recommended place to work out in Pula, noted for its welcoming atmosphere and personal approach.
